req.title: Read patient (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care; reading outside their assignment shall be denied. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-027 governs read access to Patient resources in fhir-lite. Three orthogonal concerns drive test selection: (1) a clinician assigned to a patient must receive the resource and trigger an audit entry — covered by an EP positive test on the happy path; (2) a clinician NOT assigned must be denied with HTTP 403 — covered by a NEG negative test; (3) boundary numeric/timestamp elements on the Patient resource (e.g., birthDate at epoch boundary, extreme age values) must round-trip correctly — covered by a BVA boundary test. A fourth DT test adds a decision-table angle covering the cross-product of assignment × token validity to ensure both conditions must hold simultaneously. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down their own data.

tests.name: test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_200_and_audit
preconditions: Patient 'pt_001' exists and is assigned to 'dr_assigned'
Valid Bearer token for 'dr_assigned' is available
actions: Query audit log for the access event
expected: Audit log contains one entry for clinician 'dr_assigned' reading 'pt_001'
trace: R-HLT-027: clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care
tests.executable:
def test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_200_and_audit(
    assigned_clinician_token, assigned_patient_id, audit_log_client, fhir_client
):
    resp = fhir_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{assigned_patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {assigned_clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Patient"
    assert body["id"] == assigned_patient_id

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.query(
        action="read",
        resource_id=assigned_patient_id,
        actor=<assigned_clinician_username>,
    )
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1
    assert audit_entries[-1]["outcome"] == "success"

tests.name: test_unassigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_403
preconditions: Patient 'pt_002' exists and is NOT assigned to 'dr_unassigned'
Valid Bearer token for 'dr_unassigned' is available
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome or error indicator
No Patient resource data exposed in response
trace: R-HLT-027: reading outside their assignment shall be denied
R-HLT-027: error pathway — 403 when clinician not assigned
tests.executable:
def test_unassigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_403(
    unassigned_clinician_token, unassigned_patient_id, fhir_client
):
    resp = fhir_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{unassigned_patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {unassigned_clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "resourceType" not in body or body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    assert "birthDate" not in body
    assert "name" not in body

tests.name: test_patient_boundary_birthdate_epoch_and_extreme_age_round_trip
preconditions: Patient 'pt_bva' exists with birthDate='1900-01-01' (epoch boundary) and is assigned to 'dr_boundary'
Valid Bearer token for 'dr_boundary' is available
actions: Extract birthDate and compute derived age
Verify birthDate string format and value precision
expected: birthDate field equals '1900-01-01' exactly (no truncation or timezone shift)
Derived age is a non-negative integer not exceeding 200
No numeric overflow or null in age-related extension fields
trace: R-HLT-027: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
tests.executable:
import re
from datetime import date

def test_patient_boundary_birthdate_epoch_and_extreme_age_round_trip(
    boundary_clinician_token, boundary_patient_id, fhir_client
):
    resp = fhir_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{boundary_patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {boundary_clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()

    birth_date_str = body.get("birthDate", "")
    assert re.fullmatch(r"\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}", birth_date_str), (
        f"birthDate format invalid: {birth_date_str!r}"
    )
    assert birth_date_str == "1900-01-01"

    birth = date.fromisoformat(birth_date_str)
    today = date.today()
    age_years = (today - birth).days // 365
    assert 0 <= age_years <= 200, f"Age out of plausible range: {age_years}"

    for ext in body.get("extension", []):
        if "age" in ext.get("url", "").lower():
            val = ext.get("valueInteger") or ext.get("valueDecimal")
            assert val is not None, "Null in age extension"
            assert val >= 0, "Negative age value"

tests.name: test_expired_token_for_assigned_clinician_returns_401
preconditions: Patient 'pt_001' exists and IS assigned to 'dr_assigned'
An expired Bearer token for 'dr_assigned' is available (assignment=YES, token_valid=NO)
actions: GET /Patient/pt_001 with expired Authorization header for 'dr_assigned'
expected: HTTP 401 Unauthorized (token invalid overrides assignment check)
No audit success entry written for this request
trace: R-HLT-027: DT cross-product — assigned=YES AND token_valid=NO must yield denial
R-HLT-027: error pathway — authorization prerequisite not met
tests.executable:
def test_expired_token_for_assigned_clinician_returns_401(
    expired_clinician_token, assigned_patient_id, audit_log_client, fhir_client
):
    pre_count = len(
        audit_log_client.query(action="read", resource_id=assigned_patient_id, outcome="success")
    )

    resp = fhir_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{assigned_patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {expired_clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    body = resp.json()
    assert "birthDate" not in body
    assert "name" not in body

    post_count = len(
        audit_log_client.query(action="read", resource_id=assigned_patient_id, outcome="success")
    )
    assert post_count == pre_count, "Audit success count must not increase on auth failure"
